title: S-Trig consumer-admission contract
description: Specifies the consumer trigger for a routed payload P_{i->j}: arity, component types, standing hypotheses, interface data, and certificate handles are checked before CTj starts. The record operationalizes the S-Trig consumer-trigger lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Trig`.

payloadId.description: Required nonempty identifier of the incoming payload, exactly matching its producer's S-Rout record.

consumerTacticId.description: Required nonempty identifier of the consumer node or tactic whose declared input signature is being checked.

expectedArity.description: Required nonnegative number of positional components in the consumer's trigger signature; it must equal both the routed tuple length and the number of component checks.

componentChecks.description: Required positional type-check records for the incoming tuple. Supply exactly one entry for each position from 1 through `expectedArity`, in tuple order; an empty array is appropriate only when the expected arity is zero.

items.description: One positional comparison between the consumer's expected sort and the producer's actual payload component.

position.description: Required one-based tuple position being checked; positions must collectively cover the full trigger signature without duplication or gaps.

expectedSort.description: Required consumer-declared mathematical sort at this position, including any residual-class, interface, external-type, ledger, budget, exact-type, rank, certificate, or admissibility refinement.

actualRef.description: Required reference to the actual routed tuple entry whose declared sort and proof record are compared with `expectedSort`.

valid.description: Required literal `true`, asserting that the actual component inhabits the expected sort; incompatible or unproved components make the trigger fail rather than producing a branch.

standingHypotheses.description: Required list of hypotheses CTj needs and the incoming branch state is proved to satisfy. Include H_0 assumptions, exclusions E, invariants I, residual conditions R, vocabulary entries V, payload facts Q, audit facts A, ledger/slack records, and admission assumptions as applicable; use an empty list only if the consumer is unconditional.

items.description: One required hypothesis with a reference to the state field or persistence proof that supplies it.

interfaceChecks.description: Required list of verified interface compatibilities, including boundary label sets, degree profiles, gluing conventions, context classes, or shared support/remainder identities required by the consumer.

items.description: One interface equality or compatibility assertion and its supporting reference.

certificateHandleChecks.description: Required list of verified proof/certificate handles on which the consumer relies, such as definitions, persistence records, comparison results, finite-enumeration evidence, or prior closure exclusions.

items.description: One required certificate handle and confirmation that it refers to a discharged obligation in the current state.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources proving that the incoming payload satisfies the complete trigger signature.

items.description: One evidence record supporting arity, component typing, hypotheses, interfaces, or certificate-handle validity.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for the consumer signature, producer route, persistence proof, typing check, or audit record.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a generated signature, routing graph, or formal artifact.

notes.description: Optional note identifying the particular trigger checks discharged by this evidence.
